INDIA GOVT – ZOHO CLOUD MIGRATION: POLICY INSIGHTS

CORE HIGHLIGHT  
- 16.68 lakh govt email IDs migrated to Zoho cloud (~₹180 Cr spend)

- Executed via NIC under MeitY

STRATEGIC SIGNIFICANCE  
- Push toward sovereign digital infrastructure  
- Govt retains full ownership of data & IP

KEY IMPLICATIONS  
- Large-scale adoption of Indian SaaS ecosystem  
- Boost for domestic cloud & enterprise software players  
- Validates “India stack” in government infra  

OUTLOOK  
- Potential for further govt digital stack localization  
- Opens opportunities for Indian SaaS in public sector  

IMPACT  
- Positive — strong tailwind for domestic cloud & SaaS ecosystem

KSH International - Company announces the appointment of Mr. Hukumchand Chandratan Lakhotiya as Chief Executive Officer (CEO) effective April 02, 2026. 

Mr. Hukumchand Lakhotiya has more than thirty years of domestic and international experience in the electrical and power sectors, including more than twenty-five years at CG Power & Industrial Systems Ltd. and Schneider Electric Pte Ltd. (Singapore).

He has held leadership roles across P&L, multi-plant operations, sales and business development, green energy, digitalization and project management.

During his tenure at CG Power, Mr. Lakhotiya led the transformer, switchgear and EPC business across Asia, as well as several regional Asian plants as well as its exports business.

Prior to joining KSH, he served as COO Waste to Energy, SAEL Industries Ltd.

**INDIAN EQUITY MARKETS (REVERSE DCF INSIGHT): KEY TAKEAWAYS**

**CORE HIGHLIGHT**  
- Market correction (~15%) has NOT meaningfully reset valuations; earnings downgrades offset price fall  

**INDEX-LEVEL VIEW**  
- Nifty-50 implied long-term FCF CAGR: ~10.5% (3rd lowest in decade)  
- Similar to March 2021 levels — but with far higher macro risks today  

**MACRO CONTEXT DIFFERENCE**  
- Current headwinds: geopolitical tensions, high crude, fiscal stress, weaker earnings cycle  
- Same valuation levels ≠ same opportunity  

**BOTTOM-UP REALITY**  
- Implied growth: 16.0% → 15.7% (almost unchanged)  
- Market fall absorbed by earnings downgrades, not valuation reset  

**EARNINGS TREND**  
- EBITDA CAGR (FY25–FY27): 35% → 19% (sharp cut)  
- 9/14 sectors saw FY27 FCF estimates downgraded  

**MARKET STRUCTURE**  
- Polarisation: stocks clustered in extremes (overvalued vs undervalued)  
- Weak middle ground (balanced growth vs valuation)  

**SECTORS – ATTRACTIVE**  
- IT: Implied growth ~6% (low), EBITDA ~11% → undervalued vs fundamentals  
- Telecom: Implied growth ~3.9% → lowest levels, stable outlook  
- Energy: Implied growth ~8% → cheap; risk is geopolitical  
- Power: Implied growth ~9% → attractive over 3–4 years  

**SECTORS – CAUTION**  
- Consumer Discretionary: Appears corrected but earnings cut deeper → still expensive  
- Staples: ~17% implied growth → not cheap  
- Cement: ~19% implied growth → above historical range  
- Consumer Tech: 40%+ implied growth → still priced for perfection  

**KEY FRAMEWORK (REVERSE DCF SIGNAL)**  
- High implied growth vs sector → ~62% underperformance probability  
- Low implied growth vs sector → ~51% outperformance probability  

**FLOWS IMPACT**  
- FII outflows causing sharper correction in high foreign-owned stocks  
- Short-term distortion; long-term valuation signals remain relevant  

**PORTFOLIO STRATEGY**  
- Overweight: Financials, IT, Telecom, Power  
- Selective exposure: Energy (with geopolitical caution)  
- Avoid broad buying in discretionary & staples  
- Focus on stock-specific opportunities  

**OUTLOOK**  
- Market is not broadly cheap — selective opportunities exist  
- Transition to stock-picker’s market  

**IMPACT**  
- Passive “buy the dip” strategy less effective  
- Alpha generation depends on valuation discipline & sector selection

- Implication: Profitability and debt metrics likely to weaken

The Strait of Hormuz is more than an oil route; it's a global lifeline that, when blocked, triggers a domino effect across every aspect of modern life.

Food Scarcity: It controls nearly half the world’s fertilizer exports. Blockages mean higher prices and lower crop yields, leading to global hunger.

Medical & Tech Crisis: We lose the by-products needed for MRI machines, semiconductors, and essential packaging.

The Green Irony: Even building wind turbines and electric cars requires the specific petroleum by-products trapped in the Gulf.

The Bottom Line: We aren't just facing a fuel shortage; we are facing a shortage of the "invisible" ingredients that keep the world fed, healthy, and moving.

• Strong consumption demand  
• NBFC-led credit expansion  
• Large industry capex and working capital needs  
• Rural credit demand improving  

**TREND ANALYSIS**  
• Credit growth remains well above nominal GDP  
• Shift visible toward unsecured & high-yield segments  
• Diversified growth reduces concentration risk  

**RISKS**  
• Asset quality concerns in unsecured segments  
• Overheating in gold loans & MSME lending  
• Interest rate sensitivity impacting demand  
• Regulatory tightening risk on NBFCs  

**MARKET STRATEGY (AAJ / KAL)**  
• Banks: Positive bias (credit growth supportive)  
• NBFCs: Strong momentum but monitor risk build-up  
• PSU Banks: Likely beneficiaries of corporate lending  
• Retail-focused lenders: Watch asset quality trends  

**KEY WATCHPOINTS**  
• Sustainability of >14% credit growth  
• Asset quality trends in retail/MSME  
• RBI regulatory stance on NBFCs  
• Interest rate trajectory  

**IMPACT**  
• Positive – Strong and broad-based credit growth supports economic momentum, though risk monitoring remains critical

**MACQUARIE ON INDIA EV: ADOPTION RISING, NEW LAUNCHES DRIVING MOMENTUM**

**CORE HIGHLIGHT**  
• BEV penetration steadily increasing in passenger vehicles  
• Premium segment expansion + new launches accelerating adoption  

**BEV PENETRATION TREND**  
• PV BEV penetration: 2.4% (CY24) → 4% (CY25)  
• Further improved to ~4–5% in Q1 CY26  
• Indicates gradual but consistent EV adoption  

**SEGMENT SHIFT (₹20–30 LAKH BAND)**  
• Share increased from 9% (CY24) → 24% (CY25) → 36% (Q1 CY26)  
• Premium EV segment seeing strong traction  
• Driven by better product offerings & features  

**KEY DRIVERS**  
• Multiple new launches in ₹20–30 lakh segment  
• 3 models from M&M  
• 1 model each from Tata Motors, Hyundai, and Maruti  
• Bigger battery packs addressing range anxiety concerns  

**INDUSTRY CHALLENGES**  
• Charging infrastructure ramp-up remains a key bottleneck  
• Limited EV-specific models; reliance on ICE-converted platforms  
• Availability constraints impacting adoption pace  

**OUTLOOK (12–24 MONTHS)**  
• BEV adoption expected to gradually rise  
• Improved product mix + infrastructure development key triggers

• M&M: Best positioned among PV peers in BEV space  
• Strong pipeline of EV launches supports growth visibility  
• Target Price: ₹4,168  

**2W EV SEGMENT**  
• TVS Motor strengthening EV leadership

**RISKS**  
• Weak demand environment  
• Commodity price-led margin pressure  
• Intensifying competition in EV space  

**MARKET STRATEGY (AAJ / KAL)**  
• Auto EV players: Positive bias (especially M&M, TVS)  
• Watch execution of new EV launches  
• Infra development remains key monitorable  

**KEY WATCHPOINTS**  
• EV charging infrastructure expansion  
• New EV model pipeline & acceptance  
• Battery cost trends  
• Competitive intensity  

**IMPACT**  
• Positive – Structural EV adoption trend intact, led by premium segment expansion and new launches

text:
**JEFFERIES ON CEMENT: COST PRESSURE BUILDS, PRICE HIKES LIKELY AHEAD**

**CORE HIGHLIGHT**  
• Cement prices largely stable MoM in March  
• Modest improvement of +1.5% QoQ in Q4FY26  
• Strong volume growth trend continues  

**DEMAND & VOLUME TRENDS**  
• Q4FY26 volume growth remains healthy  
• Builds on ~9% YoY growth seen in 3QFY26  
• Demand environment supportive across regions  

**COST PRESSURES**  
• Cost per tonne increased sharply by ~₹300/Tn  
• International coal prices ↑ 18% QoQ  
• US petcoke prices ↑ 12% QoQ  
• Packing costs surged 40–50% due to material shortages  

**PRICING DYNAMICS**  
• Industry needs ~5–7% price hike (₹20–25 per bag)  
• Required to offset rising input costs  
• Dealers indicate attempts of ₹15–50 per bag hikes in April  

**INDUSTRY ACTION SIGNALS**  
• Price hikes expected in April–May period  
• Industry pushing aggressively to pass on cost inflation  
• Success of hikes remains key monitorable  

**MARGIN OUTLOOK**  
• Margins under pressure due to cost spike  
• Recovery dependent on successful price hikes  
• Near-term profitability remains sensitive to input costs  

**MARKET STRATEGY (AAJ / KAL)**  
• Cement stocks: Watch for price hike execution  
• Cost inflation remains key overhang  
• Volume growth supportive but margins at risk  

**KEY WATCHPOINTS**  
• Success of April–May price hikes  
• Trend in coal & petcoke prices  
• Packing material cost normalization  
• Demand momentum sustainability  

**IMPACT**  
• Neutral to Slightly Negative – Strong demand but rising costs may pressure margins unless price hikes sustain

🚨 India’s $4 Billion MRO Opportunity: From Dependency to Strategic Control

India has long relied on foreign countries for maintaining its defence platforms — with 80–90% of MRO work done overseas.

That is now changing.

📌 What is MRO & Why It Matters?
MRO (Maintenance, Repair & Overhaul) ensures operational readiness of aircraft, ships, and submarines.

⚠️ Without domestic MRO:
– Delays in repairs
– High costs
– Strategic vulnerability in conflict

📊 Massive Demand Build-Up

India’s fleet expansion is driving demand:

✈️ IAF: ~1,800 aircraft by 2030
🚢 Navy: 200 warships, submarines doubling
🛫 Civil aviation: ~1,500 aircraft

📈 MRO market:
– $1.2B (2025) → $4B by 2030
– 27% CAGR
– Defence = 60% of growth

💰 Policy Push (Budget 2026)

₹5,000 Cr dedicated MRO package:

✔️ 100% tax holiday (5 yrs)
✔️ GST exemptions on spares
✔️ 150% depreciation on capex
✔️ 60% localization mandate in defence MRO
✔️ ₹12,000 Cr+ FDI commitments (GE, Safran, Honeywell)

🏗️ Infrastructure is Now Real

🔹 Cochin Shipyard mega dock operational
🔹 Mazagon Dock & Hindustan Shipyard expanding
🔹 Engine MRO by Rolls Royce, GE, Tata JV
🔹 HAL composite repair capabilities

👉 India is building full-stack MRO infra across air & naval segments

🌍 Big Strategic Shifts (2026)

✔️ Karwar submarine test facility (global exclusivity)
✔️ Vietnam submarine refit deal → entry into $2B regional market
✔️ Indo-Pacific Refit Alliance (QUAD-linked opportunity)

⚙️ Economics Are Attractive

💸 ₹15,000 Cr annual forex savings potential
💰 Engine overhaul: ₹50 Cr per visit
🚢 Carrier refit: ₹1,000 Cr

📉 AI + digital twins → 30% lifecycle cost reduction

🧩 Challenges Ahead

⚠️ OEM certifications (especially submarines)
⚠️ Skilled workforce gap (1.2M needed by 2030)
⚠️ Fragmented state-level ecosystem
⚠️ Trust-building for classified defence work

📌 Final Insight

India has moved from intent → execution:

✔️ Policy ready
✔️ Infra operational
✔️ FDI committed
✔️ First export contracts signed

👉 The real test now:
Can India build trust and become the Indo-Pacific’s MRO hub?

🚨 FROM ORDERS TO OPPORTUNITY: CRYOGENIC OGS ENTERS BIG LEAGUE

• EIL approval unlocked → entry into PSU tenders & larger project pipeline

• Oil & Gas + LNG infra play riding global energy demand & war-driven capex

• Order inflow momentum strong: ~₹8.1 Cr + $231K secured

• ₹5.55 Cr LOI + ₹2.28 Cr order from Emerson

• International validation via Honeywell order (~₹1.9 Cr)

• Promoters accumulating: ~1.08 lakh shares bought in Feb–Mar

• Buying at ₹159–₹163 hints strong internal confidence

• Small cap → scaling into serious infra opportunity 🚀

**VIKRAM SOLAR: BACKWARD INTEGRATION LED SOLAR PLAY WITH CYCLICAL CAPEX → FCF INFLECTION**

**CORE THESIS**  
• Transition: Module player → Integrated module + cell manufacturer  
• Capacity: Modules 9.5GW → 15.5GW; Cells 12GW (from FY27)  
• Earnings CAGR: Revenue/EBITDA/PAT ~68%/81%/110% (FY25–28)  
• Margin rebuild post FY27 via DCR mix + in-house cells  

**GROWTH DRIVERS**  
• Strong solar demand: ~200GW additions (FY25–30)  
• Policy tailwinds: BCD, ALMM, DCR, PLI push localisation  
• DCR mix: ~36% volume → ~47% revenue (FY28)  
• Module volumes: 3.2GW → 9.3GW (FY26–28 ramp-up)  

**STRUCTURAL EDGE**  
• Backward integration into cells improves realisations  
• Positioned in DCR (domestic content) premium segment  
• Beneficiary of India’s localisation push vs China dependence  

**FINANCIAL PROFILE**  
• Revenue: ₹34bn → ₹162bn (FY25–28E)  
• EBITDA: ₹4.9bn → ₹29.3bn  
• PAT: ₹1.4bn → ₹13bn  
• RoE: ~16.6% → ~29% | RoIC: ~14% → ~19%  
• EBITDA/Wp: ₹2.8 → ₹1.9 → ₹3.2 (dip then recovery)  

**CAPEX & FCF INFLECTION**  
• Heavy capex: ~₹78bn (FY26–28), peak in FY27  
• FCF negative during expansion phase  
• Strong FCF generation expected from FY28 onward  

**VALUATION**  
• TP: ₹290 (~52% upside)  
• Valuation: 8x FY28E EPS (~50% discount to peers)  
• Discount factors: Late cell entry + lower integration + execution risk  
• Re-rating dependent on execution & margin delivery  

**KEY RISKS**  
• Execution risk in 12GW cell commissioning  
• Module oversupply → pricing pressure  
• Input volatility (poly, glass, silver, aluminium)  
• Policy changes (BCD/ALMM/DCR)  

**IMPACT**  
• Positive (High Growth, Execution-Heavy) – Strong earnings inflection story, but success hinges on timely integration and margin recovery

**SHRIRAM FINANCE: HIGH-YIELD NBFC WITH ROA EXPANSION STORY**

**CORE THESIS**  
• Diversified, secured retail NBFC with strong used-vehicle & MSME franchise  
• AUM CAGR ~18% (FY26–28E) with PV, MSME & gold as key drivers  
• RoA expansion: ~3.1% → ~3.5–3.8% led by NIM improvement  
• Liability transformation post MUFG → structural CoF reduction  

**GROWTH DRIVERS**  
• AUM: ₹3.0 tn → ₹4.2 tn  
• PV & MSME: ~20–25% CAGR  
• Gold loans scaling rapidly (strong rural demand)  
• New vehicle share rising (cross-sell + lifecycle play)  

**STRUCTURAL EDGE**  
• Leadership in used vehicle financing (~97% used assets)  
• Strong rural/semi-urban underwriting franchise  
• Secured granular book (~90%+)  
• Deep OEM/customer ecosystem  

**FINANCIAL PROFILE**  
• Yield: ~16.5%+ | NIM: ~9–9.7%  
• RoA: ~3.5–3.8% | RoE: ~14%  
• Credit cost: trending <2%  
• Strong provisioning buffer (ECL ~5.9%)  

**VALUATION**  
• TP: ₹1,150 (~27% upside)  
• P/E: 17.5x → 13.3x (FY26–28E)  
• P/B: 2.7x → 1.7x  
• Re-rating driven by RoA expansion + lower CoF  

**KEY RISKS**  
• CV/MSME cycle slowdown  
• Crude/geopolitical shocks impacting borrowers  
• MSME/CE credit cost volatility  
• Rising competition in used vehicle financing  

**IMPACT**  
• Positive – Strong RoA expansion with cyclical risk exposure

**MSUMI: OPERATING LEVERAGE + CONTENT GROWTH + EV OPTIONALITY STORY**

**CORE THESIS**  
• Business model: High operating leverage + rising content per vehicle  
• Revenue CAGR: ~13% (FY26–28) driven by PV, 2W and EV growth  
• Earnings CAGR: ~27% with strong margin expansion  
• Greenfield ramp-up: Key driver of profitability and RoCE improvement  

**KEY GROWTH DRIVERS**  
• Greenfield utilization: ~45% → 65–70% (FY27) → further upside in FY28  
• Content growth:  
  – PV: ~1.7x increase (EV + premiumisation)  
  – 2W: ~3x increase with EV transition  
• Segment growth: PV & 2W outperform CV and agri/off-road  
• Strong OEM pipeline visibility due to dominant market position  

**STRUCTURAL EDGE (MOAT)**  
• Market leadership: >55% PV harness share, >25% in 2W  
• High customer stickiness with OEM integration  
• Sumitomo partnership → access to advanced systems (ECUs, junction boxes, EV solutions)  
• 30 manufacturing facilities with fungible capacity (flexibility across programs)  

**VALUE CREATION MODEL**  
• Operating leverage from utilization ramp-up  
• Content per vehicle expansion → structural revenue growth  
• Mix improvement (premium vehicles + EVs) boosts margins  
• Automation + disciplined capex enhances RoIC  

**OPTIONALITY (HIDDEN VALUE)**  
• EV transition: Multi-year structural content increase  
• Premiumisation (shift to UVs): ~1.5x content uplift in PVs  
• Advanced electronics integration → move up value chain (beyond wiring harness)  

**FINANCIAL TRAJECTORY**  
• Revenue: ₹111.7bn → ₹143.0bn (FY26–28)  
• EBITDA: ₹10.6bn → ₹16.1bn (~20–26% growth)  
• EBITDA margin: 9.5% → 11.3%  
• PAT: ₹6.1bn → ₹9.9bn  
• EPS growth: 23.7% (FY27E), 31.0% (FY28E)  
• RoE: 41–50% | RoCE: 49–60% (best-in-class returns)  

**BUSINESS MIX & OPERATING LEVERAGE**  
• Revenue mix:  
  – PV ~62% (core driver)  
  – 2W ~13–14% (fastest growth)  
  – CV ~9–10%, Agri ~6%, Others ~8–9%  
• Key plants: Navagam (Gujarat), Pune, Kharkhoda → utilization ramp critical  
• Capex: ~₹2bn annually with strong FCF (₹5.1bn → ₹9.3bn)  
• High payout ratio (~85%) supported by strong cash flows  

**COST STRUCTURE & RISKS (INPUT SIDE)**  
• Gross margin stable: ~33–34%  
• Cost mix: Employee ~17–18%, Other expenses ~6–7%  
• Copper exposure: ~10% of revenue  
  – Pass-through: 60–70% (3 months), balance (6 months lag)  
• Imports: ~27–28% of RM (limited near-term disruption risk)  

**VALUATION**  
• P/E: 41x / 33x / 25x (FY26E / FY27E / FY28E)  
• EV/EBITDA: 23.9x → 15.6x (compression with growth)  
• Target Price: ₹60 (40x FY28E EPS)  
• Premium justified by:  
  – High visibility growth  
  – Strong RoE/RoCE  
  – Structural EV tailwinds  

**KEY RISKS**  
• Weak domestic auto demand  
• Competitive intensity (global players like Aptiv, Yazaki)  
• Commodity volatility (copper) with lagged pass-through  
• Currency fluctuations impacting imports  

**WHERE ASSUMPTIONS ARE AGGRESSIVE**  
• Sustained double-digit volume growth across segments  
• Smooth Greenfield ramp without delays  
• Margin expansion despite RM volatility  
• Continued premium valuation multiples  

**STRATEGIC VIEW**  
• Not a commoditized harness player → evolving into system solutions provider  
• EV + premiumisation = structural, not cyclical, growth  
• High operating leverage + content growth creates strong earnings compounding  

**WHAT TO TRACK (CRITICAL)**  
• Greenfield utilization trajectory (45% → 70%+)  
• EBITDA margin expansion vs guidance  
• EV penetration and content per vehicle trend  
• Copper price movement and pass-through efficiency  

**IMPACT**  
• Positive (High Quality Compounder) – Strong structural tailwinds, high return ratios, but valuation leaves limited margin for error

**LLOYDS METALS: MINING → STEEL INTEGRATION STORY WITH HIGH OPERATING LEVERAGE**

**CORE THESIS**  
• Transition: Merchant miner → Integrated steel player  
• Revenue: ₹67K Cr → ₹224K Cr (FY25–FY28, ~3.3x growth)  
• EBITDA: ₹17K Cr → ₹78K Cr (massive operating leverage)  
• Margin expansion: ~29% → ~35% (mix + cost advantage)  

**KEY GROWTH DRIVERS**  
• Surjagarh mine ramp-up: 10 → 26 MTPA (core engine)  
• Pellet capacity: 4 → 12 MTPA (value addition)  
• Wire rod mill (FY27): 1.2 MTPA high-margin steel  
• Future HRC plant (FY29): next growth leg  

**STRUCTURAL EDGE (MOAT)**  
• Captive iron ore cost: ~₹1,933/t vs ₹5,500–6,000/t market  
• Logistics edge: slurry pipeline (₹850–1,250/t savings)  
• Captive power → energy stability  
• Integrated model → higher EBITDA/t  

**VALUE CREATION MODEL**  
• Ore → Pellets → Steel = margin ladder  
• EBITDA/t expands significantly across value chain  
• Mix improvement (not just price cycle) drives earnings  

**OPTIONALITY (HIDDEN VALUE)**  
• Thriveni (79.8% stake):  
  – MDO business → annuity-like cash flows  
  – Value contribution: ~₹209/share  
• Copper JV (DRC):  
  – Not valued in SOTP (pure upside optionality)  

**FINANCIAL TRAJECTORY**  
• Revenue CAGR: ~33% (FY24–29)  
• EBITDA margin: ~26% → ~35%  
• PAT: ₹12K Cr → ₹64K Cr  
• ROE moderates but remains strong (~27–35%)  
• Net debt peak manageable (~1x EBITDA target)  

**VALUATION (SOTP)**  
• Standalone: ₹1,522/sh (12x FY28 EV/EBITDA)  
• Thriveni: ₹209/sh  
• Total TP: ₹1,730 (≈32% upside)  
• Valuation compresses sharply with earnings growth  

**KEY RISKS**  
• Single-asset dependency (Surjagarh mine)  
• Execution risk (multiple projects simultaneously)  
• High capex cycle → balance sheet stress  
• Commodity cycle (steel/pellet spreads)  
• Regulatory & ESG risks (mining + DRC exposure)  

**WHERE ASSUMPTIONS ARE AGGRESSIVE**  
• Sustained ~35% EBITDA margins  
• Smooth execution of all capacity ramps  
• Strong steel spreads without major downcycle  
• High ROE despite heavy capex  

**STRATEGIC VIEW**  
• Not a pure mining play → emerging integrated steel story  
• Cost leadership + integration = structural advantage  
• Thriveni adds hidden value & cash flow stability  

**WHAT TO TRACK (CRITICAL)**  
• Ore ramp-up: 20 → 26 MTPA execution  
• Pellet + steel spreads (EBITDA/t)  
• Wire rod commissioning timeline  
• Net debt vs EBITDA discipline  

**IMPACT**  
• Positive (High Conviction but High Risk) – Strong structural story, but execution & cycle sensitivity remain key

Buying the Future, Not Nostalgia

​The attached article serves as a sobering "reality check" for investors who equate a company's past glory with its future returns. While giants like Asian Paints, HUL, and TCS remain fundamentally strong businesses, the data from 2019 to 2026 highlights a critical lesson: valuation acts as gravity. When quality stocks are bid up to "priced-for-perfection" levels—sometimes exceeding 70x or 80x earnings—even solid profit growth can result in "lost decades" or paltry returns as multiples inevitably compress.

​The shift from the 2009–2019 "golden era" to the current slowdown proves that a great company can be a poor investment if the entry price ignores a margin of safety. As interest rates normalized and liquidity tightened, the market stopped paying for "nostalgia" and began demanding tangible earnings acceleration to justify high premiums. For the modern investor, the takeaway is clear: dominance in the market does not guarantee dominance in your portfolio if you overpay for the privilege of owning it.

**No rare earths = no missiles = no war**

Even before the war started, RE stockpiles were low, and China, the biggest exporter, restricted exports on key military-linked REs

- Neodymium: 90% comes from China
- Gallium: 95% of supplies come from China
- Graphite: 80% of supplies come from China
- Tungsten: 80% of supplies come from China

One could say no China = no war.

This puts the US in a rather uncomfortable situation in which their military relies on the final endboss they plan to defeat

NBFC SECTOR: NOMURA – FUEL SHOCK MAY TRIGGER CREDIT RISK CYCLE

KEY TAKEAWAYS  
• Rising fuel costs disrupting MSME-heavy industrial belts (Gujarat, TN, Maharashtra)  
• Early signs of stress in MSME lending segment  
• Risk of spillover into vehicle finance & broader credit cycle  

STRESS BUILD-UP (CORE ISSUE)  
• MSME loans (~24% mix) under pressure  
• Fuel shortages + higher costs → cashflow disruption  
• Likely impact:  
  – Higher credit costs  
  – Slower loan disbursals  

SECOND-ORDER IMPACT  
• Lower economic activity → reduced transport demand  
• Vehicle finance segment next at risk:  
  – Fewer trips → weaker borrower cashflows  
  – Rising delinquencies possible  

MARGINS & FUNDING PRESSURE  
• Cost of funds rising (G-sec yields ~6.5%+)  
• Margin pressure across NBFCs  
• Lending growth may turn cautious  

SECTOR DIVERGENCE  
• Better placed:  
  – Housing finance  
  – Diversified NBFCs  
  – Gold loan players (defensive, secured)  

• Higher risk:  
  – MSME-focused lenders  
  – CV/vehicle finance-heavy NBFCs  

STRATEGIC VIEW  
• Stage 1: MSME stress emerging  
• Stage 2: Vehicle finance stress may follow  
• Early signs of credit cycle turning due to fuel shock  

IMPACT  
• Near-term: Negative for MSME & vehicle finance NBFCs  
• Relative positive: Housing & secured lending players remain resilient

**EICHER MOTORS: MOSL UPGRADES TO ‘NEUTRAL’ – DEMAND STABLE, EXPORTS & RISKS WEIGH**

**CALL SUMMARY**  
• Rating: NEUTRAL (upgrade)  
• Target Price: ₹6,960  
• Stock corrected ~17% → valuations now fair  

**DEMAND TRENDS**  
• Strong domestic momentum in 350cc segment (demand > supply)  
• 350cc+ segment saw slowdown post GST hike, now gradually recovering  
• Product interventions by Royal Enfield aiding demand revival  

**EXPORT & RISK FACTORS**  
• Export outlook mixed:  
  – Weakness in Europe, US & Thailand (key markets ~50% exports)  
• Geopolitical risk:  
  – Potential gas supply disruption → production risk in near term  

**STRATEGIC VIEW**  
• Focus on profitable volume growth over margin maximization  
• Domestic demand remains key growth driver  
• Valuations at ~29.8x FY27E / ~26x FY28E → fair pricing  

**IMPACT**  
• Neutral – Strong domestic demand offset by export weakness & near-term risks

**EMMVEE PHOTOVOLTAIC: KOTAK INITIATES ‘ADD’ – STRONG GROWTH WITH FUTURE MARGIN RISK**

**CALL SUMMARY**  
• Rating: ADD  
• Target Price: ₹250  
• Valuations seen as attractive; execution on track  

**BUSINESS & GROWTH DRIVERS**  
• Among India’s largest integrated solar cell & module manufacturers  
• Rapid capacity expansion planned over next 3 years  
• Growth supported by:  
  – Domestic capacity addition  
  – Higher backward integration  
  – Improved capacity utilization  

**EARNINGS OUTLOOK**  
• Expected PAT CAGR: ~44% (FY25–30E)  
• Strong earnings visibility driven by scale & integration benefits  

**RISKS & MARGIN OUTLOOK**  
• Rising competition in solar manufacturing  
• Margin & return ratios likely to moderate from FY28 onwards  

**STRATEGIC VIEW**  
• Positioned to benefit from India’s solar manufacturing push  
• Integration + scale = key competitive advantage  
• However, long-term profitability depends on industry discipline  

**IMPACT**  
• Positive – Strong growth visibility with attractive valuations, though margin risks emerge in later years

DDR Ram Prices Falling as Google expects AI memory demand could be reduced by 6x

Google released TurboQuant on March 24, a compression algorithm that cuts AI memory requirements by 6x. SK Hynix and Samsung stocks dropped 6% and 5% overnight. Corsair kits fell $60-100 from their highs within days.

**BANKING & FX: JEFFERIES – RBI NOP CAP MAY TRIGGER MTM LOSSES**

**KEY DEVELOPMENT**  
• RBI caps Net Open Position (NOP) in onshore FX at $100mn  
• Earlier: Board-driven limits (~25% of capital)  
• Surprise move tightening forex exposure norms  

**RATIONALE (WHY RBI ACTED)**  
• Sharp INR depreciation  
• Widening gap between:  
  – Offshore (NDF) market  
  – Onshore FX market  
• Aim: Stabilize INR & curb speculative positioning  

**IMPACT ON BANKS**  
• Forced unwinding of large FX positions by April 10  
• Estimated positions: $30–40bn across large & foreign banks  
• Likely MTM losses in 4QFY26 due to:  
  – Rapid position unwinding  
  – Adverse currency movements  

**SECTOR IMPLICATIONS**  
• Near-term earnings pressure for banks  
• Treasury income volatility expected  
• Large banks & foreign banks most impacted  

**STRATEGIC VIEW**  
• Positive for INR stability (macro control)  
• Negative for bank profitability (short term)  
• Regulatory tightening indicates rising FX risk concern  

**IMPACT**  
• Short-term: Negative (MTM losses, treasury hit)  
• Medium-term: Stabilizing INR may reduce systemic risk

**OMCs & ENERGY: CITI – POLICY RELIEF FOR OMCs, MIXED IMPACT ACROSS VALUE CHAIN**

**KEY POLICY CHANGES**  
• ₹10/litre excise duty cut on petrol & diesel (not passed to consumers)  
• Export duty imposed:  
  – Diesel: ₹21.5/litre  
  – Jet fuel (ATF): ₹29.5/litre  

**IMPACT ON OMCs (POSITIVE)**  
• Duty cut retained → reduces marketing losses  
• Provides meaningful earnings relief amid high crude environment  
• Improves near-term profitability visibility  

**IMPACT ON REFINERS (NEGATIVE FOR RIL)**  
• Export duties cap refining upside  
• Lower realizations on exported diesel & ATF  
• Impact depends on:  
  – Domestic vs export mix  
  – SEZ refinery exemption (await clarity)  

**UPSTREAM (KEY POSITIVE – ONGC)**  
• No windfall tax imposed  
• Allows full benefit from elevated crude prices  
• Positive deviation from past policy actions  

**STRATEGIC VIEW**  
• Government balancing act:  
  – Support OMC profitability  
  – Limit windfall gains for refiners  
• Upstream emerges as key beneficiary in current cycle  

**IMPACT**  
• OMCs: Positive (reduced losses)  
• Refiners (RIL): Negative (capped margins)  
• Upstream (ONGC): Positive (full crude upside retained)

**OMCs: GOLDMAN SACHS – RELIEF LIMITED, STRUCTURAL PRESSURE CONTINUES**

**KEY TAKEAWAYS**  
• ₹10/litre excise duty cut offers some relief, but not enough to offset high crude  
• Earnings pressure remains elevated across OMCs  
• EBITDA loss run-rate still above peak CY22 stress levels  

**CRUDE VS BREAKEVEN (CORE ISSUE)**  
• Pre-conflict breakeven: ~$70/bbl (Dubai crude)  
• Adjusted for weak INR: ~$67/bbl  
• Post duty cut: ~$78/bbl  
• Current crude: ~$122/bbl → still far above breakeven  

**COMPANY-WISE VIEW**  
• IOC:  
  – Rating: SELL  
  – Least favourable risk-reward  
  – Downside: ~49% (bear) / ~20% (base)  

• HPCL:  
  – Partial support from diesel export tax (lower domestic prices)  
  – Still facing margin pressure  
  – Rating: NEUTRAL  

• BPCL:  
  – Similar margin challenges  
  – Rating: NEUTRAL  

**FORWARD OUTLOOK**  
• CY27 Brent estimate: ~$80/bbl  
• Even with normalization:  
  – Risk-reward remains constrained  
  – Limited pricing flexibility caps upside  

**STRATEGIC VIEW**  
• High crude + policy constraints = structural profitability pressure  
• OMC earnings remain highly sensitive to:  
  – Crude volatility  
  – Government intervention  

**IMPACT**  
• Negative bias – Elevated losses and weak risk-reward despite policy relief

**LG ELECTRONICS: GOLDMAN SACHS – STRONG GROWTH OUTLOOK, BUT NEAR-TERM PRESSURES**

**CALL SUMMARY**  
• Rating: BUY  
• Target Price: ₹1,730 (cut from ₹1,740)  
• Positive long-term view despite minor estimate revisions  

**GROWTH & MARGINS OUTLOOK**  
• FY26: Expected double-digit revenue growth  
• Margins: Double-digit margin profile sustained for full year  
• Export mix target: >20% by 2030  

**EARNINGS REVISION**  
• Earnings estimates trimmed due to:  
  – Demand softness  
  – Cost pressures (input + operating)  

**STRATEGIC VIEW**  
• Strong structural story driven by:  
  – Premiumization  
  – Export expansion  
• Near-term headwinds manageable within long-term growth trajectory  

**IMPACT**  
• Positive (Long-term) – Growth & margin profile intact  
• Short-term: Slight negative due to demand & cost pressures

**QSR SECTOR: GOLDMAN SACHS – LPG DISRUPTION POSES NEAR-TERM MARGIN RISK**

**KEY TAKEAWAYS**  
• LPG supply constraints impacting QSR operations  
• Jubilant FoodWorks seeing noticeable disruption; peers impacted to a lesser extent  
• Issue appears sector-wide, not company-specific  

**OPERATIONAL & COST IMPACT**  
• Shift to alternate fuels → higher operating costs  
• Input cost inflation likely across QSR players  
• Short-term margin pressure expected  

**DEMAND & BUSINESS RISK**  
• Risk of operational disruption if shortage persists  
• Potential second-order impact:  
  – Lower store throughput  
  – Demand slowdown during peak consumption periods  

**STRATEGIC VIEW**  
• Near-term headwind for entire QSR sector  
• Severity depends on duration of LPG shortage  
• Players with better energy flexibility (PNG/electricity) relatively better placed  

**IMPACT**  
• Short-term: Negative (cost inflation + operational risk)  
• Medium-term: Normalization expected once supply stabilizes

**JUBILANT FOODWORKS: JPMORGAN ‘NEUTRAL’ – GEOPOLITICAL RISKS EMERGING**

**CALL SUMMARY**  
• Rating: NEUTRAL (downgrade in stance)  
• Target Price: ₹500 (cut)  
• View turns cautious amid rising external uncertainties  

**KEY RISKS (WEST ASIA IMPACT)**  
• LPG supply disruption affecting store operations  
• Potential slowdown in discretionary consumption  
• Both factors linked to ongoing West Asia conflict  

**EARNINGS IMPACT**  
• Near-term: Marginal impact in March’26  
• Forward risk: Meaningful impact likely in Q1FY27 if disruption continues  
• Visibility remains low due to evolving situation  

**ESTIMATE CUTS**  
• FY27E Revenue: ↓ 2%  
• FY27E EBITDA: ↓ 7%  
• Target multiple reduced to reflect higher uncertainty  

**STRATEGIC VIEW**  
• Dual pressure:  
  – Operational disruption (LPG)  
  – Demand-side risk (consumer slowdown)  
• Unlike temporary issue, macro uncertainty adds layered risk  

**IMPACT**  
• Near-term: Negative bias with earnings downgrade cycle  
• Medium-term: Recovery dependent on normalization of supply & demand environment

**JUBILANT FOODWORKS: MORGAN STANLEY ‘OVERWEIGHT’ – SHORT-TERM LPG DISRUPTION**

**CALL SUMMARY (MORGAN STANLEY)**  
• Rating: OVERWEIGHT (OW)  
• Target Price: ₹690  
• Investment View: Structural growth intact despite near-term disruption  

**KEY ISSUE (EXTERNAL HEADWIND)**  
• LPG cylinder supply constrained in certain store locations  
• Impacting kitchen operations across parts of network  
• Unprecedented operational disruption  

**COMPANY RESPONSE**  
• Taking corrective measures:  
  – Conserving LPG usage  
  – Shifting to alternate energy sources:  
    • Electricity  
    • Piped Natural Gas (PNG)  

**NEAR-TERM IMPACT (4QFY26)**  
• Likely temporary impact on:  
  – Store operations  
  – Sales throughput  
  – Margins (due to alternate energy costs)  

**SEASONAL RISK (KEY MONITORABLE)**  
• IPL window: March 28 – May 31 (peak demand period)  
• If LPG constraints persist:  
  – Impact may extend into 1QFY27  
  – Potential revenue loss during high consumption season  

**STRATEGIC INSIGHT**  
• Issue is external & temporary, not structural  
• Business fundamentals remain intact:  
  – Strong brand (Domino’s)  
  – Scalable delivery-led model  
• Transition to PNG/electricity may reduce future dependency on LPG  

**RISKS**  
• Prolonged LPG supply disruption  
• Higher operating costs due to alternate energy  
• Demand impact during peak IPL season  

**IMPACT**  
• Neutral to Slightly Negative (Short-term) – Temporary operational headwind; long-term growth story remains unchanged

**AVENUE SUPERMART (DMART): CLSA HIGH-CONVICTION ‘OUTPERFORM’ – STORE EXPANSION ACCELERATES**

**CALL SUMMARY (CLSA)**  
• Rating: High Conviction OUTPERFORM  
• Target Price: ₹6,185  
• Investment Thesis: Strong store addition momentum driving growth visibility  

**STORE EXPANSION (KEY HIGHLIGHT)**  
• 9 new stores opened in last 3 days  
• Total store count: 481  
• Aggressive scale-up across geographies  

**ANNUAL STORE ADDITIONS (ABOVE EXPECTATION)**  
• FY store additions: 66 stores  
• CLSA expectation: ~60 stores  
• Beat: ~10% higher than expectations  

**QUARTERLY MOMENTUM (STRONG EXECUTION)**  
• Stores added this quarter: 39 (with 2 days remaining)  
• Same quarter last year: 28 stores  
• Indicates sharp acceleration in expansion  

**GROWTH IMPLICATIONS**  
• Faster store rollout → higher revenue visibility  
• Scale benefits to improve operating leverage  
• Strengthens leadership in value retail segment  

**STRATEGIC INSIGHT**  
• DMart continuing its disciplined yet accelerated expansion strategy  
• Focus remains on:  
  – High asset efficiency  
  – Cluster-based expansion  
  – Strong inventory control  

**RISKS**  
• Execution risk with rapid store additions  
• Margin pressure if expansion outpaces demand  
• Competition from quick commerce & modern retail  

**IMPACT**  
• Positive – Strong store addition momentum reinforces growth visibility and supports premium valuation narrative

**INDIA ENERGY: GOLDMAN SACHS VIEW – POLICY SHIFT & MARGIN PRESSURES**

**KEY POLICY ACTIONS**  
• Excise duty cut by ₹10/litre on petrol & diesel  
• Windfall tax reinstated on diesel & ATF exports  
• Government balancing inflation control vs fiscal revenue  

**CRUDE OIL CONTEXT (CORE PRESSURE)**  
• OMC breakeven crude: ~$78/barrel  
• Current crude: ~$122/barrel  
• Implies: Significant margin stress across downstream players  

**OMCs (OIL MARKETING COMPANIES) VIEW**  
• IOC: Least favourable risk-reward among OMCs  
• HPCL & BPCL: Rated Neutral  
• Key concern:  
  – High crude vs capped retail pricing  
  – Margin compression risk  

**REFINING & EXPORT DYNAMICS**  
• Windfall tax impacts export realizations  
• RIL refining margins likely impacted  
• Policy overhang reduces upside from high global cracks  

**GLOBAL PRODUCT CRACKS (SUPPORTIVE BUT LIMITED BENEFIT)**  
• Diesel cracks: ~$75/barrel (elevated)  
• Jet fuel cracks: ~$46/barrel  
• However: Export taxes dilute benefit for refiners  

**RIL OUTLOOK**  
• FY27 refining EBITDA estimate: ~$12/barrel  
• Moderation expected due to:  
  – Export tax impact  
  – Volatile crude-product spreads  

**STRATEGIC INSIGHT**  
• High crude = structural negative for OMCs  
• Government intervention caps profitability upside  
• Refiners face policy risk despite strong global spreads  

**RISKS**  
• Sustained high crude prices  
• Further policy intervention (taxes, price controls)  
• Volatility in global demand & refining margins  

**IMPACT**  
• Negative bias for OMCs due to margin pressure & policy overhang  
• Mixed for refiners like RIL – strong cracks offset by taxation risk

**HDFC BANK: JPMORGAN UPGRADES TO ‘OVERWEIGHT’ – DEEP VALUE EMERGING**

**CALL SUMMARY (JPMORGAN)**  
• Rating: UPGRADE to OVERWEIGHT (from Neutral)  
• Target Price: ₹1,010  
• CMP: ₹758  

**VALUATION RE-RATING OPPORTUNITY**  
• Trading at ~1.5x FY28E P/B  
• Lowest valuation since merger announcement  
• At ~16-year historical low valuation band  
• 24% YTD correction → sharp valuation compression  

**UNDERPERFORMANCE CONTEXT**  
• Stock has underperformed:  
  – Banking peers  
  – Broader markets  
• Market concerns already priced in:  
  – Earnings moderation  
  – Post-merger integration challenges  

**UPGRADE RATIONALE (KEY DRIVERS)**  
• Expected recovery in system-wide credit growth  
• Shift in flows toward large-cap quality banks  
• HDFC Bank well positioned to capture incremental demand  
• Strong franchise + liability strength remains intact  

**EARNINGS & RISK FACTORING**  
• Recent de-rating already factors in:  
  – Earnings risks  
  – Margin pressure  
• Downside risk limited at current valuation levels  

**STRATEGIC INSIGHT**  
• Classic case of:  
  – Strong franchise  
  – Temporary earnings headwinds  
  – Deep valuation comfort  
• Positioned for:  
  – Gradual earnings normalization  
  – Re-rating as growth visibility improves  

**IMPACT**  
• Positive – Attractive risk-reward with valuation comfort and potential upside from earnings recovery + sector tailwinds

**L&T FINANCE: MOSL REITERATES ‘BUY’ – STRONG RETAIL TRANSFORMATION PLAY**

**CALL SUMMARY (MOTILAL OSWAL)**  
• Rating: BUY  
• Target Price: ₹370  
• Investment Thesis: Transition to retail-focused, tech-driven NBFC improving growth, asset quality & profitability  

**BUSINESS TRANSFORMATION (CORE STORY)**  
• Shift from wholesale → retail-focused lending model  
• Technology-led underwriting & risk management  
• Better customer mix driving sustainable growth  

**GROWTH OUTLOOK**  
• AUM growth guidance: 20–25%  
• Focus segments:  
  – Gold loans  
  – Micro LAP (Loan Against Property)  
  – Housing finance  
• Expansion strategy: ~400 new gold loan branches  

**PROFITABILITY DRIVERS**  
• PAT CAGR expected ~32%  
• Return ratios improving:  
  – RoA: ~2.8%–3%  
• Operating leverage to kick in with scale  

**MARGINS & FUNDING**  
• NIM + fees guided at ~10%–10.5%  
• Stable margins:  
  – Lower cost of funds offsets yield pressure  

**ASSET QUALITY IMPROVEMENT**  
• Credit cost expected to reduce:  
  – From ~2.8% → ~2.2%  
• Collection efficiency: >99%  
• Shift toward secured & prime lending improving risk profile  

**SEGMENT-WISE INSIGHTS**  
• Gold Loans: High-yield + rapid scale-up  
• Micro LAP: Strong growth opportunity in underserved segment  
• Housing Finance: Stable long-term growth driver  
• Two-Wheeler Finance: Shift to prime borrowers → lower risk  
• Tractor Finance: Profitability improving sharply  

**TECHNOLOGY EDGE (KEY DIFFERENTIATOR)**  
• AI platforms:  
  – Cyclops  
  – Nostradamus  
• Benefits:  
  – Better underwriting accuracy  
  – Lower credit costs  
  – Faster decision-making  

**COST STRUCTURE**  
• Short-term: Elevated opex due to tech investments  
• Long-term: Cost-to-income to improve with scale benefits  

**RECENT PERFORMANCE (Q3 FY26)**  
• Net Profit: ₹739 Cr (↑ 18% YoY)  
• Strong earnings driven by:  
  – Retail mix improvement  
  – Better asset quality  
  – Stable margins  

**STRATEGIC INSIGHT**  
• L&T Finance moving toward:  
  – High-yield retail NBFC model  
  – Tech-led underwriting moat  
• Positioned to deliver:  
  – Superior growth + improving asset quality  

**RISKS**  
• Execution risk in rapid retail expansion  
• Higher near-term operating costs  
• Competitive intensity in gold loans & retail lending  

**IMPACT**  
• Positive – Strong earnings visibility, improving RoA, and scalable retail model support rerating potential

text:
Jal Jeevan Mission has enabled massive scale-up. Last-mile challenges remain

India has pulled off a quiet transformation under the mission with tap water coverage surging from 16.7% (2019) to 81.8% (2026), with several states achieving 100% household connections. A huge infrastructure leap in just a few years.

But the challenge of reliable supply yet to be addressed. Even among “fully covered” states, functionality lags with some states well below 50%.

1 in 6 households (16.9%) report insufficient water quantity. Quality concerns are far lower (7.6% dissatisfied)

India receives $138B annual remittances vs lower FDI inflows, with diaspora income at $700B globally. Remittances nearly doubled in 10 years and finance ~50% of merchandise trade deficit. ~35M diaspora send ~$13,000 every 3 seconds, with >70% expecting stable or higher flows.

**MOTILAL OSWAL: TOP STOCK PICKS**

**KEY HIGHLIGHTS**  
• bullish on select sector leaders  
• Picks span healthcare IT, infrastructure, aviation, and asset management  

**TOP PICKS & UPSIDE**  
• Sagility India Ltd: CMP ₹40 → Target ₹58 (Upside: ~45%)  
• Kalpataru Projects International Ltd: CMP ₹1,110 → Target ₹1,500 (Upside: ~35%)  
• InterGlobe Aviation Ltd: CMP ₹4,295 → Target ₹5,500 (Upside: ~28%)  
• ICICI Prudential Asset Management Company Ltd: CMP ₹2,901 → Target ₹3,500 (Upside: ~21%)  

**GROWTH DRIVERS**  
• Healthcare IT: Outsourcing and digital healthcare demand  
• Infrastructure: Strong order books and execution visibility  
• Aviation: Demand recovery and pricing power  
• AMC: Rising financialization and mutual fund penetration  

**STRATEGIC VIEW**  
• Focus on sector leaders with strong earnings visibility  
• Balanced exposure across cyclical and structural growth themes  

**IMPACT**  
• Positive – Broad-based opportunities across key sectors with attractive upside potential

Don't expect
To get what you give
Not every one
Has a Heart like you.
Aequs - Initiating Coverage - JM Financial
